title: Delhi High Court allows Sonam Wangchuk to shift to private hospital
content: The court was hearing an appeal filed by Wangchuk’s wife Gitanjali Angmo, challenging a Single Judge’s refusal to allow his transfer to a private hospital of his choice

title: Political rivalries clash over Karnataka’s Bidadi township project
content: The Bidadi township project envisages acquiring about 9,600 acres across 25 villages in the Bengaluru South district.

title: Bengaluru: Delivery agent held for stripping inside woman’s flat
content: The delivery agent allegedly entered the apartment without permission and requested to use the washroom. About two minutes later, he emerged naked and exposed himself to her.

title: Adult unmarried daughter can claim financial support for education from father under DV Act: Karnataka High Court
content: In a landmark ruling, the Karnataka High Court has affirmed that an adult unmarried daughter can seek financial support from her father for educational expenses under the Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.

title: Parliament Monsoon Session LIVE: Both Houses adjourned for the day amid Oppn. protests seeking discussion on CJP march crackdown
content: LoP Rahul Gandhi along with some Opposition MPs met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, urging him to allow a discussion in the House on the “brutality unleashed” on students

title: Defections affect moral sanctity of democracy
content: The greatest victim of political defections is not the Opposition party that loses a legislator. It is the voter

title: CJP protest in Delhi LIVE: Delhi High Court allows Wangchuk to shift to Medanta Hospital
content: Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda met with doctors in Delhi's Ram Manohar Lohia hospital to enquire about the injured; CJP leader Abhijeet Dipke apologises to supporters who were 'brutally beaten' by police

title: Rain drenches Hyderabad on June 20 and 21, several Telangana districts receive intense showers
content: The IMD predicts light to moderate rain or thundershowers at isolated places in Hyderabad and its surrounding areas on July 21

title: Dream voyage ends in tragedy for Keralite as missile strike kills merchant navy officer weeks after wedding plans
content: Akhil Joyan, a 26-year-old merchant navy officer from Kerala, was killed in a Russian missile strike on the MV Golden Leo near Odessa, Ukraine, just 40 days after returning to sea with plans to marry his fiancée next April.

title: Six-month UDF rule in Kerala’s Pala municipality ends with ouster of chairperson Diya Binu
content: No-confidence motion, moved by LDF against Diya Binu, regarded as youngest chairperson of any civic body in the country, was carried with support of 17 councillors, including four Congress members and UDF vice-chairperson

title: AIIMS doctors appeal to President over inquiry into events at Jantar Mantar, Wangchuk's hospitalisation
content: Calling itself an apolitical association, the RDA said, "We believe every individual has the democratic right to peaceful protest, and we hope that all genuine concerns are heard and addressed through constructive dialogue”

title: CM Vijay requests Ford to resume production operations in Tamil Nadu
content: A delegation led by Ford’s vice-president, Matthew Godlewski, held discussions with the Chief Minister at the Secretariat inChennai on July 20, 2026

title: PM Modi must apologise, resign along with Shah: Rahul on police crackdown on CJP protest
content: Mr. Gandhi further said that the testing system has been hollowed out by termites and that is what the students are stating

title: NSA Ajit Doval meets Saudi Foreign Minister, discusses energy security and regional situation
content: NSA Ajit Doval’s visit comes amid heightened tensions in West Asia, with concerns over disruptions to key shipping routes and energy supplies following the continuing conflict between the U.S. and Iran

title: July 21, 1993 Kolkata police firing: What does the Commission of Inquiry report reveal? | Explained
content: The long-pending report has opened a fresh political debate over the July 21, 1993 police firing in Kolkata. Here is why it matters

title: Assam flood situation eases, death toll rises to 11
content: An official update says 3.11 lakh people remain affected across 11 districts, down from 16 on July 20

title: Nara Lokesh distributes land titles to 3,000 residents in Mangalagiri
content: The Minister said the titles, covering lands worth about ₹1,000 crore, would give long-time occupants in Mangalagiri and Tadepalli mandals legal ownership and the ability to raise bank loans

title: Odessa ship attack: Bodies of two Indian seafarers identified
content: Chief Officer Akhil Jovan and Ordinary Seaman Abhishek Nishad among confirmed casualties; eight bodies still unidentified due to severe burns

title: Lokayukta raids properties of Madhya Pradesh DISCOM engineer, uncovers assets, expenditure worth ₹7.16 crore
content: Lokayukta searches across nine locations uncovering assets of the accused DISCOM engineer with properties registered under names of family members, said senior official

title: TVK government in Tamil Nadu faces a fiscal test in balancing populism and prudence
content: “We can have a welfare State only if we work for it and step up production,” said the then Chief Minister C. Rajagopalachari in June 1952

title: PM Modi outlined steps to ensure no paper leaks in future: Rijiju
content: Minister Kiren Rijiju on said Prime Minister Narendra Modi has said that the government took immediate action after the NEET paper leak, promising students’ futures

title: Flyovers in Hyderabad’s IT corridor busy junctions targeted for completion by May 2027
content: Foundation works, piers and pier caps have reached advanced stages at IIIT junction. The works are under progress at Khajaguda junction.

title: Flies infesting Kotagiri, Coonoor not the harmful ‘black flies’: researchers
content: Since last week, local residents have been complaining of the fly infestation, stating that they are causing eye irritation, while economic activity has also been affected, especially at eateries and restaurants.

title: V.S. Achuthanandan death anniversary: Pinarayi Vijayan says former CM and communist leader remains ‘a blazing memory’
content: In a Facebook post, Pinarayi pays tribute to VS and says the latter aggressively championed people’s causes, strong-arming governments to correct course

title: Rahul Gandhi, Oppn. MPs meet Lok Sabha Speaker; seek discussion on 'brutality' on students during CJP protest march
content: "The Opposition will not let this be buried. We will ensure that the students' voice will be heard on the streets, and in Parliament," the LoP in Lok Sabha said

title: SC to consider Sena (UBT) plea against Speaker’s approval of MPs’ ‘unilateral merger’
content: Lawyer argued that ‘merger’, a device originally meant to safeguard legislators’ rights of expression and intra-party dissent from the anti-defection law, has metamorphosed into a cover for horsetrading

title: TVK government’s first Budget to be presented on August 5 in Tamil Nadu
content: Minister for Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare Vinoth will present the State’s Agriculture Budget on August 6, 2026

title: India’s Index of Core Industries gets an updated series: What has changed? | Explained
content: The Centre has released a revised series of the ICI, updating the base year, sectoral coverage and weights. Here is what has changed and what it means for industrial growth

title: Krishna Byre Gowda targets ‘paper plantation’ in Bengaluru
content: According to the Minister, the civic body has not been planting trees as per its claims and has been showing inflated numbers on paper owing to a lack of accountability and improper documentation

title: CDSCO clears recombinant live attenuated dengue vaccine for ages 4 to 60 years
content: ‘The approval has been granted after a comprehensive scientific evaluation of the vaccine’s quality, safety and efficacy,’ said the Union Health Ministry

title: West Bengal Martyr’s Day 2026: What is it?
content: West Bengal marked Martyr’s Day on July 21, 2026, with significant rallies in Central Kolkata, drawing attention from political leaders including Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari and former Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee.

title: BLOs in Karnataka threatened to raise SIR progress from 60% to 75% in a day or face action
content: The notices, issued by the Assistant Electoral Registration Officers on July 20, asked the BLOs to complete 75% of the enumeration work by July 21 – within a day – or face disciplinary action.

title: SIR in Karnataka: Voters above 85 years face double checks, say BLOs
content: Booth Level Officers say they have been instructed to place them under the “probable absent, shifted, dead/deleted or duplicate (ASDD)” category, Chief Electoral Officer denies claims.

title: Kerala State Planning Board reconstituted with K.M. Chandrasekhar as vice chairperson
content: Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan is chairperson. Chandrasekhar was vice chairperson of the board during the term of Oommen Chandy-led UDF government from 2011-16

title: AMMA row: Shwetha Menon says ‘will go to any extent’ to ensure ‘power group’ does not lead association
content: In a social media post, the actor says her fight is against the ‘power group’ named in Hema Committee report

title: What makes Skyroot’s Vikram-1 launch unique? | Explained
content: Skyroot’s Vikram-1 has made India only the third country where a private company has independently placed payloads in orbit. Here’s what makes the mission significant

title: How will U.S. visa policy changes affect students, media professionals, and exchange visitors | Explained
content: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security cites national security risks from Indian and Chinese students and introduces changes in F, J and I categories of visas. PhD scholars, journalists, and exchange visitors will be impacted

title: From luxury villas to gold, Telangana ACB sharpens focus on public servants’ disproportionate assets
content: Record DA cases, rising public complaints and high-profile raids underscore the Telangana ACB’s growing focus on disproportionate assets alongside routine bribery

title: Ten killed in NHPC tunnel collapse in Sikkim’s Namchi district; rescue operations under way
content: According to reports, a methane gas explosion triggered the tragedy at the tunnel at Samardung in the district’s Jholungey area

title: AI in skill education to replace third language in select high schools; BEOs instructed to appoint one ‘AI guest teacher’ per school in Karnataka
content: The Block Education Officer concerned has been instructed to appoint one ‘AI guest teacher’ per school to teach AI

title: IED destroyed in controlled blast in J&K's Rajouri
content: The IED was noticed by an army search party in village Argi Dalhori late Monday (July 20, 2026) evening, the officials said

title: India summons Russian envoy over killing of four Indian seafarers near Odessa, Ukraine
content: The Ministry of External Affairs said that it has conveyed “India’s grave concerns and unequivocal condemnation” over the attack to the Russia envoy

title: 5,289 cyber awareness programmes held across Gujarat
content: The campaign focuses on preventing cybercrime through community engagement, technology-driven surveillance, victim outreach, cyber awareness and prompt legal action

title: Karnataka HC dismisses PIL complaining procedural lapse in one notification of acquiring 518 acres for Bidadi township project
content: However, court clarifies that this order is not an expression of opinion on other issues related to acquisition notifications

title: Woman-founded Amaravati startup Bringgg secures ₹40 lakh NRI investment
content: Logistics startup Bringgg aims to create a peer-to-peer logistics network that connects travellers, senders and seekers while helping reduce or offset travel costs

title: BJP protest in Belagavi alleging delay in declaring drought in Karnataka
content: Protestors walked from Kittur Channamma Circle to the office of the Deputy Commissioner, and shouted slogans against the government

title: Minister seeks to rope in RWAs in sapling plantation drive ‘Bengaluru Hasirooru’ to be launched on August 15
content: To meet the target, the GBA corporations have collectively earmarked ₹9.64 crore in their budgets, covering both plantation and years of maintenance

title: From IAC to paper leaks: Why anti-system movements pose a unique political challenge
content: The IAC movement consciously projected itself as a citizens’ campaign, even though the Congress-led UPA maintained that it represented a collaboration between the RSS and civil society activists

title: Martyrs’ Day 2026 turns out to be show of political strength for rival Trinamool factions in West Bengal
content: Trinamool Congress MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar, who, along with 19 other party Lok Sabha MPs, has merged with the little-known Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI), said the faction would observe Martyrs’ Day at Rajghat

title: HC allows Wangchuk's transfer to private hospital; Centre gives 'no objection' with a condition
content: Sonam Wangchuk received Delhi High Court permission to move hospitals. He is on a hunger strike for the alleged NEET paper leak. The court ordered his transfer to Medanta Hospital for continued treatment. Safdarjung Hospital will provide all necessary medical treatment details. A panel of doctors will oversee his care at the new facility.

title: 'Completely un-Indian': Rahul demands PM Modi's apology over students' protest crackdown
content: Rahul Gandhi criticised Prime Minister Modi's silence on police action against students.  Opposition leaders met the Lok Sabha Speaker demanding a parliamentary discussion.  Protesters and police accused each other of violence during demonstrations.  Students are protesting against the education and testing system's failures.  The opposition vows to ensure students' voices are heard in Parliament.

title: ‘We are eating leaves’: Assam floods make boy spend night on tree, others on rooftops
content: Torrential runoff from Nagaland triggered devastating floods across Assam's Sivasagar and Charaideo districts. Thousands of families were marooned, with many taking refuge on rooftops and trees. A viral video highlighted the plight of a boy and his family surviving on leaves. Former MLA Debabrata Saikia described the situation as terrifying and critical. Many stranded individuals faced challenges reaching authorities due to communication failures.

title: India Test squad for Lanka tour likely this week; selectors sweat over Sundar
content: India's selectors are set to pick the squad for the two-Test tour of Sri Lanka, with Washington Sundar's hamstring injury the biggest selection concern. Jasprit Bumrah and Ravindra Jadeja are expected to return, while decisions on Sundar's replacement and the all-rounder balance remain pending. India need a strong series to boost their World Test Championship qualification hopes.

title: SBI Funds Management listing makes some employees crorepatis
content: SBI Funds Management made a strong debut on Dalal Street today, listing at a premium of around 7% over its IPO price. The stock opened at Rs 610 on the BSE, which is a gain of 6.27% from the issue price of Rs 574, while it listed at Rs 613.30 on the NSE.

title: Google employees' union gives ‘never-before-delivered' petition to CEO Sundar Pichai
content: Nearly 100 Google employees rallied at Mountain View, delivering a petition signed by 4,500 workers to CEO Sundar Pichai. Led by Alphabet Workers Union president Parul Koul, the Googlers for Job Security campaign demands guaranteed severance, voluntary exit packages before layoffs, and an end to AI-era performance quotas. Executives were absent, so the petition was slipped under their doors amid rising tech layoffs anxiety.

title: Bike mechanic's faulty repair damaged man's motorcycle; consumer court awards Rs 66,000
content: A Kerala consumer commission ordered a mechanic to pay over sixty-six thousand rupees.  This decision followed a daily wage labourer's motorcycle breaking down shortly after repairs.  The mechanic had quoted ten thousand rupees but took twelve thousand in advance for the work.  The commission found the repair work negligent and a deficiency in service provided.  The labourer suffered financial losses and mental agony due to the faulty repairs.

title: 'British govt wouldn't have lathi-charged like this': Kejriwal on CJP protest
content: Arvind Kejriwal condemned the Centre's police action against protesters during a march. Videos showed police using force against unarmed students, including young women. Kejriwal alleged the Modi government's actions were unprecedented and brutal. The Aam Aadmi Party launched a helpline for affected students and protesters. This follows clashes and detentions during the march towards Parliament.

title: Jeff Bezos on returning to work after retirement: I just couldn’t sit on sidelines
content: Jeff Bezos has returned to an operational role to launch his new AI startup, Prometheus.  He describes this demanding work as rewarding, which he calls "Type 2 fun."  Bezos believes artificial intelligence will create a labor shortage, not job losses.  His new venture has secured significant funding and is now his primary focus.  He is energized by the long road ahead as Prometheus scales its operations.

title: Native Australian grass used for millennia reveals unusual chromosome secret
content: Channel millet, a native grass, possesses twelve chromosome copies, a trait linked to domestication.  Mithaka people utilized this grain for millennia, demonstrating sophisticated ecological knowledge.  This desert plant thrives in Channel Country's extreme boom-and-bust cycles.  Its genetic makeup suggests resilience, offering potential for future climate-resilient crops.  Ancient Aboriginal practices shaped native grains, revealing hidden strengths for modern food systems.

title: UK landlord firm fined £42,500 after tenants found near dead rats and waste
content: A landlord company in County Durham has been fined £42,500 after tenants were found living in unsafe conditions, including sleeping among dead rats, mice, human waste and urine. The court heard the firm failed to carry out required property inspections and maintain valid gas safety certificates across several rental homes, prompting legal action by Durham County Council.

title: Satya Nadella flips a Nobel economist's paradox warning AI buyers about a hidden cost
content: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has flipped Nobel economist Kenneth Arrow's Information Paradox to warn AI buyers about a hidden cost. In his "Reverse Information Paradox" essay, Nadella says enterprises pay for intelligence twice—with money and proprietary knowledge. As firms feed AI models their expertise, the seller learns more while the buyer learns little in return.

title: 'Future of youth priority': PM Modi says NEET leak national issue, asks to avoid 'partisan politics'
content: More than 170 people, including CJP protesters and Delhi Police personnel, were treated at central government hospitals on Monday following clashes during the 'Chalo Sansad' march, with a young woman admitted to Safdarjung Hospital in critical condition, sources said. According to hospital sources, around 150 injured people were taken to Dr Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital, while at least 24 others received treatment at Lady Hardinge Medical College (LHMC).

title: Unmanned choppers, India's Varun and now Anduril's Thunder to change how we think about drones
content: Anduril Industries unveiled its autonomous attack rotorcraft Thunder at the Farnborough Airshow. This unmanned system is designed as a drone wingman for current and future attack helicopters. India also advanced its indigenous unmanned systems with the Varun Mk I Naval Shipborne UAS. Both developments highlight how unmanned rotary systems will become central to modern warfare. These systems offer flexibility and reduced risk to human crews in combat.

title: Controversy! Ronaldo likes Insta post claiming FIFA helped Argentina reach final
content: Cristiano Ronaldo's Instagram account liked a post alleging FIFA aided Argentina's World Cup final journey. This incident adds to existing tournament controversies and questions about FIFA's integrity. Argentina's path to the final involved several close matches and dramatic comebacks. They ultimately lost the World Cup final to Spain after extra time. Ronaldo has not publicly commented on his social media activity.

title: Dipke apologises to supporters, says he could have done more to protect them
content: The Cockroach Janta Party alleged police used excessive force during their recent march.  Protesters claim volunteers were beaten and seriously injured by male officers.  The party leader apologized to supporters for the alleged police brutality.  He stated the movement would continue until Dharmendra Pradhan resigns from his position.  Delhi Police, however, reported protesters turned violent and caused public property damage.

title: 'Picked up in car, sexually assaulted, filmed': Woman 'gang-raped' at Karnataka hotel
content: Mysuru police have detained three youths from T Narasipura in connection with an alleged gang rape. The woman, reportedly from West Bengal, was moved to a rehabilitation centre for support. Police registered a gang-rape case under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita based on the survivor's complaint. Preliminary inquiry suggests the woman voluntarily travelled with the accused to the hotel.

title: Watch: US firefighters rescue dog that went missing after July 4th fireworks
content: Firefighters rescued a dog swimming in San Francisco Bay after it fled fireworks.  The lost canine was located using binoculars and a drone by the fire district.  After being found half a mile offshore, the dog was taken to the SPCA.  The animal welfare organization then reunited the scared pet with its grateful family.  The owner expressed deep gratitude for the successful rescue operation.

title: Delhi University tightens campus safety norms before new session
content: Ahead of the 2026–27 academic session, Delhi University has directed its colleges and affiliated institutions to tighten anti-ragging measures and strengthen campus discipline. The guidelines call for enhanced surveillance, confidential grievance mechanisms, faculty mentoring for freshers, identity verification and strict compliance with UGC regulations to ensure a safe, secure and inclusive learning environment for students.

title: New warning on oil: Goldman Sachs says crude prices could cross $120
content: In a note dated July 20, Goldman Sachs analysts said, “Escalation in the Middle East and the decline in estimated Persian Gulf flows to below 45% of pre-war levels have pushed oil prices back up.”

title: Sonu Sood, Vir Das, Dia Mirza, Soha Ali Khan Back CJP’s Student Protest Over NEET Row
content: Numerous film industry figures rallied in support of the CJP youth protest at Jantar Mantar in Delhi. Students raised their voices against perceived NEET irregularities and called for sweeping reforms in the education sector. Tensions escalated as protesters skirmished with security forces in their quest to reach Parliament. Celebrities emphasized the need for compassion and constructive dialogue, denouncing aggressive tactics while urging reforms and the resignation of concerned ministers.

title: 'Direct violation': Canada vows to protect workers after Trump slaps 50% tariffs on select goods
content: Canadian Prime Minister Carney stated new US tariffs violate the CUSMA trade agreement.  He affirmed continued government support for Canadian workers and businesses.  These tariffs target Canadian exports like vehicles and dairy products.  Canada has proposed detailed solutions and remains open to negotiations.  The nation will take necessary measures to strengthen its domestic economy.

title: India summons Russian diplomat over 4 Indians killed in strike off Ukrainian coast
content: Four Indian nationals were killed after a commercial vessel, MV Golden Leo, came under attack while departing Ukraine's port of Odesa on Sunday, the Indian government confirmed. One more Indian crew member remains in critical condition.  According to the government, the vessel had 17 crew members on board, including five Indian nationals, when it was attacked.

title: Gold price prediction today: Why are gold prices under pressure?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices continue to be under pressure and downside risks remain, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: Navy to commission submarine hunter Malvan as China, Pakistan expand underwater fleet
content: The Indian Navy commissions the anti-submarine warfare vessel Malvan, adding to its fleet. This new ship is the second of eight Mahe-class vessels being built domestically. The navy plans to induct eighteen vessels this year, including other specialized ships. These shallow water craft are designed to counter underwater threats near the coast. The commissioning occurs amid rising submarine activity from China and Pakistan.

title: Can a housing society recover the previous owner's dues from a new buyer? Explained
content: It's essential for buyers to check for outstanding maintenance dues linked to a flat before making a purchase. Apartment societies have the right to claim any unpaid charges from new owners. Local laws and society regulations govern who is responsible for these dues. Under certain agreements, buyers can recover any paid amounts from the previous owner, so securing a no-dues statement in writing is vital before closing the deal.

title: 'Lokan di avaaz rab di avaaz': Diljit Dosanjh backs CJP protesters
content: Singer Diljit Dosanjh supported student protesters after a police crackdown in Delhi.  He urged authorities to listen to the students' demands and concerns.  The Cockroach Janta Party accused police of excessive force during their march.  Activist Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike after witnessing the events.  Protests at Jantar Mantar persisted despite overnight rain and challenging conditions.

title: Qatar Airways denied boarding to infant despite issuing pass; court awards Rs 10 lakh
content: A Kerala consumer commission ordered Qatar Airways to pay ten lakh rupees compensation.  The airline denied boarding to two children, including an infant, despite issuing boarding passes.  This action was deemed a deficiency in service and unfair trade practice by the commission.  The family suffered significant mental agony and hardship due to the airline's arbitrary decision.  Qatar Airways must comply with the order within forty-five days.

title: Tripura DGP who had led Nirav, anti-Sikh riots probes found dead in office toilet
content: Tripura DGP Anurag Dhankar was found dead in his office toilet on Monday.  A magisterial probe has been ordered into the circumstances surrounding his death.  The government declared one day of state mourning for the respected officer.  Additional DGP Goduguluri Srinivasa Rao has been appointed as the acting police chief.  Investigators are examining all possible factors, including a potential suicide note.

title: Shades of Maradona's 1990 final in Lionel Messi's 2026 journey
content: Two geniuses, two completely different characters, yet so similar in their endeavour to defend the ultimate glory their professional lives had to offer. Messi could easily have walked away after the Qatar high. Not many would have questioned Maradona either if Argentina didn’t match the dizzying heights of 1986 four years on.

title: DHSE Kerala Plus Two SAY Result 2026 Soon: Check Result Link, Login Details
content: Kerala Plus Two SAY Result 2026: The Kerala Plus Two SAY and Improvement Result 2026 is expected to be announced today, July 21, 2026, by DHSE Kerala. Students can check their provisional scorecards through the official result websites using their register number and date of birth. Those facing website congestion can also access results through the PRD Live app, school-wise result facility or DigiLocker after declaration. Candidates must meet the prescribed subject-wise passing criteria to qualify.

title: 'Police attacked truck carrying Shabana, Prakash': CJP claims Gijanjali's hair was pulled
content: The Cockroach Janta Party alleges police brutality during a recent Parliament march.  Protesters claim public figures were assaulted and dragged from a truck.  One activist's wife reportedly had her hair pulled during the police action.  The group states their volunteer suffered shattered ribs and other injuries.  They demand the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan and government dialogue.

title: Shambhu border sealed as farmers head to Delhi to oppose India-US trade deal
content: Farmers marched towards Delhi to protest the proposed Indo-US trade agreement.  Police barricaded the Shambhu border, preventing many from reaching their destination.  Protesters voiced concerns about potential impacts on Indian agriculture and dairy sectors.  They demanded transparency and consultations regarding the trade deal.  The Desh Bachao Morcha called for the government to make agreement details public.

title: Bank refunded insurance premium after borrower's death, denied claim; family gets relief
content: A consumer commission ruled against Jammu and Kashmir Bank and PNB MetLife India Insurance. The bank reversed a loan insurance premium after the borrower's death. This action denied benefits to the deceased's family, which was deemed unfair. The commission ordered the bank and insurer to pay the insurance amount. They were also directed to compensate the family for mental agony and costs.

title: ‘Avengers: Doomsday’ trailer faces VFX backlash; Indian fans prefer ‘Ramayana’
content: The Avengers: Doomsday trailer has generated significant online discussion regarding its visual effects. Many viewers have criticized the CGI as unfinished and unpolished, while others believe it will improve. Indian fans have drawn comparisons to the Ramayana trailer, praising its visual quality. Despite the visual effects debate, demand for Avengers: Doomsday remains exceptionally high. Advance ticket sales for the film have already surpassed other major releases.

title: Sikkim tunnel landslide: Nine dead, several feared trapped as rescue operation continues
content: At least seven workers have died in a Sikkim tunnel landslide incident. Several other workers are feared trapped inside the under-construction tunnel. Rescue operations are underway by multiple agencies to evacuate those still inside. Authorities suspect around twenty-seven workers were inside when the landslide occurred. The exact cause of the accident is still under investigation by officials.

title: MP grandmother held for killing 20-day-old baby girl over desire for boy
content: A 20-day-old baby girl was allegedly strangled by her grandmother in Tikamgarh. The 60-year-old accused reportedly desired a male grandchild, police stated. She allegedly took the infant outside and committed the act while the mother slept. The grandmother then attempted to conceal the death as natural. Police arrested the accused, who is now in judicial custody.

title: Indian Tourists In Singapore Steal Diamond Worth $1,54,000, Hide It In Mouth
content: Indian national Mangroliya Manojkumar Kurjibhai jailed in Singapore after swapping a genuine diamond worth USD154,000 with a fake at a Chinatown store

title: "We're Trapped, Send Help": Woman, 2 Daughters Die In Fire At Delhi Home
content: Firefighters broke the lock and entered the house. Inside, they found three people who were burnt alive.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Not Just Jets, China Is Developing A '5-Piece' Carrier Set, And India Should Worry
content: China is now the only state serially producing two distinct stealth fighter families simultaneously, with some estimates projecting that advanced-fighter output across all jet programmes could reach 300-400 aircraft annually by 2027.

title: Andrew Tate, Brother Tristan Fight Extradition To UK Over Rape, Sex Trafficking Charges
content: The brothers are already charged with rape, bodily harm, human trafficking, and controlling prostitution for gain relating to three other alleged victims in the U.K. between 2012 and 2015.

title: Brazil To Austraia: World Shifts Towards Social Media Bans For Children
content: A proposal to ban social media for under-15 children is to be voted on by France's parliament on Tuesday.

title: Pak Man Caught Near International Border In Jammu
content: During interrogation, the individual identified himself as Mohammad Rafiq (53) alias Kala, a resident of Pindi village in Pakistan, officials said.

title: Iran Say It Hit US Military Targets In Bahrain, Kuwait
content: The announcement came as the United States said it struck Iranian military command centres, air defence systems and missile launch sites in overnight attacks.

title: China May Tighten AI Export Rules To Block Western Buyouts: Report
content: Regulators led by China's Ministry of Commerce have been consulting top homegrown AI and chipmaking companies on how to stop China's advanced technologies and leading start-ups from being acquired by the west

title: "Unacceptable": India Protests Deaths Of 4 Indian Sailors In Russian Attack Near Ukraine
content: India has condemned the attack and reiterated that targeting commercial shipping and endangering innocent civilian crew members is "deplorable and should be avoided."

title: 'More Savings, Better Spending': What PM's Economic Advisory Council Says On Direct Cash Transfer To Women
content: The report's findings indicate that women receiving the benefits maintained higher end-of-the-month bank balances compared to earlier periods.

title: 5 Killed In Apparent Mass Drowning In Ohio River In US
content: As the swimmer began to struggle in the water, two other adults tried to assist him but began floundering themselves, leading two more adults to make rescue attempts before all five disappeared in the river

title: Four-Year-Old's Scalp Torn In A Brutal Stray Dog Attack In Karnataka
content: Four-year-old Gopal was playing near his house in Maliyabad in Karnataka's Raichur city, when he was attacked by a stray dog.

title: Indian Army Rescues Over 200 From Assam Floods Under 'Operation Jal Rahat'
content: Equipped with rescue boats, medical teams, engineering resources and relief stores, Indian Army has evacuated stranded civilians from inundated areas.

title: Cybercrime Gangs Extorted Over $88 Billion In Scams From Asia-Pacific: Report
content: Criminals in Southeast Asia, an epicentre for cyberfraud by mostly Chinese-origin syndicates, are outpacing crackdowns, diversifying revenue streams, exploiting corruption and harnessing artificial intelligence.

title: Iran Targets Another Ship In Hormus Amid US Strikes On 10th Consecutive Night
content: The interim deal signed last month that was meant to end the fighting has crumbled. Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has largely stalled. And as fighting intensifies, both sides have targeted civilian infrastructure relied on by millions of people.

title: 'Iran Would Pay Many Times Over': Trump's Big Warning After US Troop Deaths
content: President Trump vowed Iran will pay dearly for every US soldier killed, as fatalities hit 17 and Houthi rebels threaten a Saudi port blockade

title: 'Won't be any more elections': Nicaragua ends hope for democratic vote
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: 'Similar insensitivity on full display after one year': Congress on Dhankhar resignation
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. title: Two premier hospital RDAs seek President's intervention over Wangchuk case
content: Anuja Jaiswal is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India, with an impressive 18-year career in narrative journalism. She specializes in health and heritage reporting, expertly simplifying complex health information to make it engaging and understandable for readers. Her deep dives into heritage topics are well-researched, resulting in captivating narratives that resonate with her audience. Over the years, she has worked in Chandigarh, Chhattisgarh and West UP, gaining diverse on-ground experience that shapes her storytelling. Read More
